title: Regime-specific ensemble with beta calibration on out-of-fold predictions
summary: The public best appears plateaued near the same CV with similar heuristic features, which is a classic sign that diversity and probability shaping may matter more than more features.
rationale: The public best appears plateaued near the same CV with similar heuristic features, which is a classic sign that diversity and probability shaping may matter more than more features. Beta calibration is specifically motivated as a more flexible alternative to Platt scaling, and regime-wise blending matches F1’s clear dry/wet and early/late-race behavior shifts.
implementation_hint: Build 2-3 diverse members: current heuristic GBDT baseline, CatBoost ordered-categorical model, and the lap-ranking model. Fit OOF beta calibrators globally or by coarse regimes such as dry vs wet and early/mid/late race, then blend with weights learned on OOF AUC/logloss rather than equal averaging.
expected_effect: Small but realistic gain from combining complementary errors and fixing distorted probability tails before submission.
risk: AUC is rank-based, so over-calibration can be neutral or harmful if it collapses ordering; regimes must stay coarse to avoid overfitting.
